title: MarginalizationAndIntervention
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem marginalize_hardInterventionOn_comm (G : CDMG Node)
    (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node) (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn W₁ hW₁).marginalize W₂
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₂ hDisj.symm)
      = (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).hardInterventionOn W₁
        (subset_carrier_of_marginalize hW₂ hW₁ hDisj)
-- claim_3_18 -- end statement
:= by
  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG Node},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  · -- J: both sides = G.J ∪ W₁
    rfl
  · -- V: (G.V \ W₁) \ W₂ = (G.V \ W₂) \ W₁
    change (G.V \ W₁) \ W₂ = (G.V \ W₂) \ W₁
    ext x
    simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff]
    tauto
  · -- E: filter equality via Φ_E iff
    exact doit_marg_E_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj
  · -- L: filter equality via Φ_L iff
    exact doit_marg_L_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj

theorem marginalize_extendingCDMGsWith_comm (G : CDMG Node)
    (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node) (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.extendingCDMGsWith W₁ hW₁).marginalize (W₂.image IntExtNode.unsplit)
        (image_unsplit_subset_extendingCDMGsWith_V hW₁ hW₂)
      = (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).extendingCDMGsWith W₁
        (subset_carrier_of_marginalize hW₂ hW₁ hDisj)
-- claim_3_18 -- end statement
:= by
  -- Field-by-field CDMG extensionality (matches Part i's pattern but over `IntExtNode Node`).
  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG (IntExtNode Node)},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  · -- J: both sides reduce to G.J.image .unsplit ∪ (W₁ \ G.J).image .intCopy
    rfl
  · -- V: image-sdiff identity
    change G.V.image IntExtNode.unsplit \ W₂.image IntExtNode.unsplit
      = (G.V \ W₂).image IntExtNode.unsplit
    exact image_unsplit_sdiff
  · -- E: lift / descend through walk-algebra; see `ext_marg_E_field_eq`.
    exact ext_marg_E_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj
  · -- L: bifurcation lift / descend; see `ext_marg_L_field_eq`.
    exact ext_marg_L_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj

theorem marginalize_nodeSplittingOn_comm (G : CDMG Node)
    (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node) (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.nodeSplittingOn W₁ hW₁).marginalize (W₂.image SplitNode.unsplit)
        (image_unsplit_subset_nodeSplittingOn_V_of_disjoint hW₁ hW₂ hDisj.symm)
      = (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).nodeSplittingOn W₁
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₁ hDisj)
-- claim_3_18 -- end statement
:= by
  -- CDMG.ext: prove four data fields agree.
  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG (SplitNode Node)},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  · -- J: marginalize and nodeSplittingOn both preserve G.J's image under .unsplit.
    rfl
  · -- V: ((G.V \ W₁).image .unsplit ∪ W₁.image .copy0 ∪ W₁.image .copy1) \ W₂.image .unsplit
    --   = ((G.V \ W₂) \ W₁).image .unsplit ∪ W₁.image .copy0 ∪ W₁.image .copy1
    -- Strategy: the W₂.image .unsplit slice only intersects the .unsplit-piece;
    -- the .copy0 / .copy1 pieces survive intact.  Then apply image_split_unsplit_sdiff
    -- and sdiff_right_comm.
    change ((G.V \ W₁).image SplitNode.unsplit ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy0
              ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy1) \ (W₂.image SplitNode.unsplit)
        = ((G.V \ W₂) \ W₁).image SplitNode.unsplit ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy0
              ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy1
    ext x
    simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff, Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_image]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨h_main, h_notW₂⟩
      rcases h_main with (h_uns_or_c0 | h_c1)
      · rcases h_uns_or_c0 with h_uns | h_c0
        · -- x ∈ (G.V \ W₁).image .unsplit
          obtain ⟨v, ⟨hvV, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩ := h_uns
          refine Or.inl (Or.inl ⟨v, ⟨⟨hvV, ?_⟩, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩)
          intro hvW₂
          exact h_notW₂ ⟨v, hvW₂, rfl⟩
        · -- x ∈ W₁.image .copy0
          exact Or.inl (Or.inr h_c0)
      · exact Or.inr h_c1
    · rintro (h_uns_or_c0 | h_c1)
      · rcases h_uns_or_c0 with h_uns | h_c0
        · -- x ∈ ((G.V \ W₂) \ W₁).image .unsplit
          obtain ⟨v, ⟨⟨hvV, hvNW₂⟩, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩ := h_uns
          refine ⟨Or.inl (Or.inl ⟨v, ⟨hvV, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩), ?_⟩
          rintro ⟨w, hw, hEq⟩
          have : w = v := by injection hEq
          exact hvNW₂ (this ▸ hw)
        · -- x ∈ W₁.image .copy0 — distinct constructor from .unsplit
          obtain ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩ := h_c0
          refine ⟨Or.inl (Or.inr ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩), ?_⟩
          rintro ⟨v, _, hEq⟩
          cases hEq
      · -- x ∈ W₁.image .copy1
        obtain ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩ := h_c1
        refine ⟨Or.inr ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
        rintro ⟨v, _, hEq⟩
        cases hEq
  · -- E: complex case analysis on edge constructors (.unsplit_pair, transfer .copy0/.copy1).
    exact split_marg_E_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj
  · -- L: bifurcation surgery (no transfer edges interact with L since L uses copy0 only).
    exact split_marg_L_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj

theorem marginalize_hardInterventionOn_comm (G : CDMG Node)
    (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node) (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.hardInterventionOn W₁ hW₁).marginalize W₂
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₂ hDisj.symm)
      = (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).hardInterventionOn W₁
        (subset_carrier_of_marginalize hW₂ hW₁ hDisj)

:= by
  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG Node},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  ·
    rfl
  ·
    change (G.V \ W₁) \ W₂ = (G.V \ W₂) \ W₁
    ext x
    simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff]
    tauto
  ·
    exact doit_marg_E_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj
  ·
    exact doit_marg_L_field_eq W₁ W₂ hW₁ hW₂ hDisj

theorem marginalize_extendingCDMGsWith_comm (G : CDMG Node)
    (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node) (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.extendingCDMGsWith W₁ hW₁).marginalize (W₂.image IntExtNode.unsplit)
        (image_unsplit_subset_extendingCDMGsWith_V hW₁ hW₂)
      = (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).extendingCDMGsWith W₁
        (subset_carrier_of_marginalize hW₂ hW₁ hDisj)

:= by

  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG (IntExtNode Node)},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  ·
    rfl
  ·
    change G.V.image IntExtNode.unsplit \ W₂.image IntExtNode.unsplit
      = (G.V \ W₂).image IntExtNode.unsplit
    exact image_unsplit_sdiff
  ·
    exact ext_marg_E_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj
  ·
    exact ext_marg_L_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj

theorem marginalize_nodeSplittingOn_comm (G : CDMG Node)
    (W₁ W₂ : Finset Node) (hW₁ : W₁ ⊆ G.V) (hW₂ : W₂ ⊆ G.V)
    (hDisj : Disjoint W₁ W₂) :
    (G.nodeSplittingOn W₁ hW₁).marginalize (W₂.image SplitNode.unsplit)
        (image_unsplit_subset_nodeSplittingOn_V_of_disjoint hW₁ hW₂ hDisj.symm)
      = (G.marginalize W₂ hW₂).nodeSplittingOn W₁
        (subset_sdiff_of_disjoint hW₁ hDisj)

:= by

  have cdmgExt : ∀ {G₁ G₂ : CDMG (SplitNode Node)},
      G₁.J = G₂.J → G₁.V = G₂.V → G₁.E = G₂.E → G₁.L = G₂.L → G₁ = G₂ := by
    rintro ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩
           ⟨_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _⟩ hJ hV hE hL
    obtain rfl := hJ; obtain rfl := hV; obtain rfl := hE; obtain rfl := hL; rfl
  refine cdmgExt ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_
  ·
    rfl
  ·

    change ((G.V \ W₁).image SplitNode.unsplit ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy0
              ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy1) \ (W₂.image SplitNode.unsplit)
        = ((G.V \ W₂) \ W₁).image SplitNode.unsplit ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy0
              ∪ W₁.image SplitNode.copy1
    ext x
    simp only [Finset.mem_sdiff, Finset.mem_union, Finset.mem_image]
    constructor
    · rintro ⟨h_main, h_notW₂⟩
      rcases h_main with (h_uns_or_c0 | h_c1)
      · rcases h_uns_or_c0 with h_uns | h_c0
        ·
          obtain ⟨v, ⟨hvV, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩ := h_uns
          refine Or.inl (Or.inl ⟨v, ⟨⟨hvV, ?_⟩, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩)
          intro hvW₂
          exact h_notW₂ ⟨v, hvW₂, rfl⟩
        ·
          exact Or.inl (Or.inr h_c0)
      · exact Or.inr h_c1
    · rintro (h_uns_or_c0 | h_c1)
      · rcases h_uns_or_c0 with h_uns | h_c0
        ·
          obtain ⟨v, ⟨⟨hvV, hvNW₂⟩, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩ := h_uns
          refine ⟨Or.inl (Or.inl ⟨v, ⟨hvV, hvNW₁⟩, rfl⟩), ?_⟩
          rintro ⟨w, hw, hEq⟩
          have : w = v := by injection hEq
          exact hvNW₂ (this ▸ hw)
        ·
          obtain ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩ := h_c0
          refine ⟨Or.inl (Or.inr ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩), ?_⟩
          rintro ⟨v, _, hEq⟩
          cases hEq
      ·
        obtain ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩ := h_c1
        refine ⟨Or.inr ⟨w, hw, rfl⟩, ?_⟩
        rintro ⟨v, _, hEq⟩
        cases hEq
  ·
    exact split_marg_E_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj
  ·
    exact split_marg_L_field_eq W₁ hW₁ W₂ hW₂ hDisj
lean_explanation:
Throughout the file, `Node : Type*` with `[DecidableEq Node]` plays the role of the ambient vertex type, matching the LN's silent assumption that the graph $G = (J, V, E, L)$ is built over some carrier; decidable equality is load-bearing because every node-set in the encoding is a `Finset Node`, and the marginalization/intervention operators construct their output sets via `Finset.filter` and `Finset.image`, both of which need a `DecidableEq` instance on the carrier.

The LN's single lemma fans out into three Lean theorems because each part lives in a different carrier:

- `marginalize_hardInterventionOn_comm` proves Part (i), an equality in `CDMG Node` (hard intervention preserves the node type);
- `marginalize_extendingCDMGsWith_comm` proves Part (ii), an equality in `CDMG (IntExtNode Node)` (adding intervention nodes introduces fresh symbols $I_w$, modeled as a sum-type carrier);
- `marginalize_nodeSplittingOn_comm` proves Part (iii), an equality in `CDMG (SplitNode Node)` (node-splitting introduces tagged copies $w^0$, $w^1$, again via a sum-type carrier).

Each theorem takes the same shape: a CDMG `G`, two finite vertex sets `W₁ W₂ : Finset Node`, the typing hypotheses on `W₁` and `W₂`, and a `Disjoint W₁ W₂` hypothesis encoding the LN side condition $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$. The typing on `W₁` is `W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` in Parts (i) and (ii) (matching $W_1 \subseteq J \cup V$) and tightened to `W₁ ⊆ G.V` in Part (iii), because `nodeSplittingOn` (`def_3_11`) requires its input to be a subset of the output nodes — the construction *removes* `W₁` from `V` and replaces it with tagged copies, which only makes sense on output nodes.

The conclusion in each part is a literal `=` of CDMGs. The two sides are well-typed only after the carrier-matching machinery is supplied: helpers like `subset_sdiff_of_disjoint`, `subset_carrier_of_marginalize`, `image_unsplit_subset_extendingCDMGsWith_V`, and `image_unsplit_subset_nodeSplittingOn_V_of_disjoint` (defined privately at the top of the file) discharge the subset side-conditions that the inner and outer operators each demand. A reader new to the Lean idioms will recognise `Finset.image IntExtNode.unsplit` and `Finset.image SplitNode.unsplit` as the canonical lifts of an original-carrier subset $W_2 \subseteq V$ into the extended carrier; `×ˢ` denotes the Finset product (used inside the field equalities for $L$ and $E$); and `Disjoint W₁ W₂` is Mathlib's lattice-theoretic disjointness predicate, which on `Finset` is propositionally equivalent to $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ via `Finset.disjoint_left`.
design_choices:
**Three theorems, not one parametric statement.** The LN bundles the three parts under a single `\begin{Lem}` heading and a closing "a similar statement holds" clause, but the three return-carriers differ — `CDMG Node`, `CDMG (IntExtNode Node)`, `CDMG (SplitNode Node)`. Lean has no natural way to host a parametric quantification over "intervention operator" that lets the return type vary with the operator, so the three-theorem shape mirrors the LN's enumeration literally. The split also matters for downstream citation: later chapters cite Part (i) alone (e.g. in the ID-algorithm manipulations $G_{\doit(C)}^{\sm B}$ and in counterfactual constructions), and bundling would obstruct selective reference.

**One Lean file, not three.** The parts share the same upstream-def chain (`def_3_1`, `def_3_10`/`_11`/`_13`, `def_3_14`), the same template helpers, and the same proof strategy (CDMG field-by-field extensionality on `J`, `V`, `E`, `L`). Splitting per-part would scatter the lift helpers across three files and force triple re-import of the same chain.

**Literal `=` of CDMGs, not an equality-up-to-isomorphism.** For each part both sides of the displayed equation are CDMGs of the same Lean type, because the operators in each part preserve a common carrier (`hardInterventionOn` preserves `Node`; `extendingCDMGsWith` and the outer `extendingCDMGsWith` both produce `CDMG (IntExtNode Node)`; analogously for node-splitting). No `eqViaNodeMap` workaround is needed.

**Inner-marginalize carrier lift via `Finset.image .unsplit`.** In Parts (ii) and (iii), the inner CDMG already lives over the extended carrier, so the outer marginalization must consume a `Finset` of the extended carrier. The natural lift of $W_2 : \mathrm{Finset}\ \mathrm{Node}$ is `W₂.image IntExtNode.unsplit` (resp. `SplitNode.unsplit`), which lands in the `.unsplit`-image slice of the extended `V`. The LN-faithful reading is that marginalization is applied to the *original* output nodes, never to fresh intervention symbols or tagged copies — and by `Disjoint W₁ W₂`, none of `W₂`'s elements has a `.copy0`/`.copy1` companion in the extended graph anyway.

**Disjointness binder oriented as `Disjoint W₁ W₂`.** This matches the LN's symmetric phrasing $W_1 \cap W_2 = \emptyset$ and aligns with the sibling rows (`claim_3_4`, `claim_3_8`, `claim_3_17`). The carrier-transport helpers consume specific orientations: where the helper takes `Disjoint W₂ W₁`, the call site composes with `.symm`. Keeping the binder in LN orientation means the theorem signature reads naturally; the orientation flips are confined to the helper-call arguments.

**Typing departure in Part (iii).** Parts (i) and (ii) take `W₁ ⊆ G.J ∪ G.V` (matching the preconditions of `hardInterventionOn` and `extendingCDMGsWith`), but Part (iii) tightens to `W₁ ⊆ G.V`. This is forced by `nodeSplittingOn` (`def_3_11`), which removes `W₁` from `V` and creates tagged copies — an operation that only makes sense on output nodes. The rewritten canonical tex statement reflects this typing distinction explicitly.

**Field-by-field equality deferred to the proof.** Equality of two CDMGs unfolds via the structure injectivity inherited from `def_3_1` to a conjunction on the four data fields `J`, `V`, `E`, `L` (the five propositional fields are discharged by proof irrelevance). The statements do not bake this unpacking in; each proof opens with a local `cdmgExt` extensionality lemma and then dispatches the four field equalities, matching the rewritten tex's closing remark.
